description: GETKINETIK neutral bureau — public read registry. Used by scripts/bureau-run.mjs. Routing is a policy engine over anomaly primitives (anomalyTaxonomy), not a per-network mapping. Networks declare an anomalyType + a concrete action (verificationCall for verify-device routes, clarificationRequest for clarify routes); the generator derives assetClass, verificationEligible, and route from the taxonomy. Routes: monitor (no action / economic-only), clarify (bounded reality-check with the operator required before any device test — observed at aggregate level, no device axis yet), verify-device (per-device ground truth exists and is device-verifiable).

duplication_cluster.note: Co-located / duplicate coordinates — sample entities and confirm distinct real device identities.

capacity_violation.note: More devices claimed than a region should hold — attest active, signed device presence.

identity_collision.note: Shared/duplicated device identifiers — confirm hardware-distinct identities.

inactivity_anomaly.note: Counted-but-inactive devices where a per-device axis already exists — conditional verification of signed heartbeat continuity. Aggregate-only observations must first pass through telemetry_discontinuity → clarify before they qualify here.

telemetry_discontinuity.note: Aggregate time-series flatline / discontinuity observed via a public feed, with no per-device ground truth yet. Requires a bounded reality-check with the operator (ETL/display freeze vs real activity change?) before any device-level verification. Not verify-device-eligible on its own — verify-device only applies once confirmation establishes a real per-device change.

economic_concentration.note: On-chain holder/economic shape — NOT a device-level claim. verify-device cannot link holders to operators; monitor only.

scaffold.note: Scan scaffolding not yet producing a validated finding.

networks.name: Geodnet
networks.scanNotes: Public RTK coverage_stations JSON — no auth.

routing.verificationCall: Sample station IDs that share an exact coordinate pair in §1 and run POST /api/verify-device on each operator's Proof of Origin to confirm whether they resolve to distinct, real device identities.
For operators in the over-capacity H3 cells in §1, run POST /api/verify-device to attest active, signed device presence over time and compare against the public cell tally.

networks.name: Hivemapper
networks.publicSource: Solana mainnet HONEY SPL mint (public RPC)
networks.scanNotes: Set SOLANA_RPC_URL in .env for reliable on-chain Part A.

networks.name: Natix Network
networks.publicSource: Natix Coverage Map metrics feed (coverage.natix.network/coverage/v1/metrics) + Solana NATIX SPL mint
networks.scanNotes: Part A reads the public Coverage Map metrics (no auth) and flags frozen cumulative output metrics vs moving driver count. Part B reads on-chain concentration (SOLANA_RPC_URL). Optional --nodes= geometry pass if a contributor snapshot is shared.

routing.clarificationRequest: Sanity-check (not a test request): ask whether the public Coverage Map metrics feed (/coverage/v1/metrics/global) is expected to stay flat in production — KM mapped / detections plateaued ~2026-05-01 while driver registrations kept rising. Offer the exact change-point timestamps + requests. verify-device only becomes relevant if confirmation establishes a real per-device activity change rather than an ETL/display freeze.

networks.name: Nodle
networks.publicSource: Nodle public scan inputs (see script header)
networks.scanNotes: Geometry heuristics on public snapshot. Flags beacon collision clusters and zero-jitter heartbeats.

routing.verificationCall: Sample flagged node IDs showing BEACON_COLLISION_CLUSTER from §1 and run POST /api/verify-device to confirm whether co-located nodes resolve to distinct, real device identities.

networks.name: Dawn Network
networks.publicSource: Dawn public scan inputs (see script header)
networks.scanNotes: Public endpoint scan. Flags hardware/appId clone twins and datacenter IP sources.

routing.verificationCall: Sample flagged app IDs showing HARDWARE_APPID_CLONE_TWIN from §1 and run POST /api/verify-device to confirm whether cloned app instances resolve to distinct, real device identities.

networks.name: Grass / Titan
networks.publicSource: Grass/Titan public scan inputs (see script header)
networks.scanNotes: May require live mode flags — see sybil-scan-grass.mjs.
